items.title_zh: 一个名为 Hunter Alpha 的神秘 1T 参数人工智能模型于 3 月 11 日出现在 OpenRouter 上，引发了 DeepSeek 正在悄悄测试其 V4 模型的猜测（Eduardo Baptista/路透社）

items.title: Alibaba raises prices for its T-Head AI computing chips, including the Zhenwu 810E, by 5% to 34%, and its Cloud Parallel File Storage by 30%, after demand soars (Luz Ding/Bloomberg)
items.title_original: Alibaba raises prices for its T-Head AI computing chips, including the Zhenwu 810E, by 5% to 34%, and its Cloud Parallel File Storage by 30%, after demand soars (Luz Ding/Bloomberg)
items.title_en: Alibaba raises prices for its T-Head AI computing chips, including the Zhenwu 810E, by 5% to 34%, and its Cloud Parallel File Storage by 30%, after demand soars (Luz Ding/Bloomberg)
items.title_zh: 在需求飙升后，阿里巴巴将包括真武 810E 在内的 T-Head AI 计算芯片的价格提高了 5% 至 34%，并将云并行文件存储的价格提高了 30%（Luz Ding/彭博社）
items.title_bilingual: 在需求飙升后，阿里巴巴将包括真武 810E 在内的 T-Head AI 计算芯片的价格提高了 5% 至 34%，并将云并行文件存储的价格提高了 30%（Luz Ding/彭博社） / Alibaba raises prices for its T-Head AI computing chips, including the Zhenwu 810E, by 5% to 34%, and its Cloud Parallel File Storage by 30%, after demand soars (Luz Ding/Bloomberg)

items.title:
R to @balajis: Btw: it’s true that there are both imperialist nationalists and autarkic nationalists.

The imperialists want to conquer other countries, while the autarkics want nothing to do with them. Both actually have a logic and an illogic.

The logic of empire is: like any business, if you're not growing you're dying. But there's also an illogic, the illogic of imperial overstretch.

Similarly, the logic of autarky is: it's good to be able to build it yourself. The illogic is when that's taken so far that there's a rejection of the division of labor intrinsic to capitalism, or the idea of learning from others.

Generally it's better to treat these political paradigms like programming paradigms. Just like there's sometimes a time for a recursive approach vs an imperative approach, there's sometimes a time for an autarkic approach. But you need not identify yourself as a recursionist or an autarkicist. Just use the best tool available.

There's also in theory a third path that's neither expansionist nor autarkic. Just be a "normal" country, trading with others, neither conquering nor hating them! In practice, though, this means you're either part of an empire or you're autarkic enough to survive outside that empire.

items.title_zh:
R 致@balajis：顺便说一句：确实，既有帝国主义民族主义者，也有自给自足的民族主义者。

帝国主义想要征服其他国家，而自给自足主义者则不想与其他国家有任何关系。两者其实都有逻辑和非逻辑。

帝国的逻辑是：像任何企业一样，如果你不成长，你就会灭亡。但也有一个不合逻辑的地方，即帝国过度扩张的不合逻辑之处。

同样，自给自足的逻辑是：能够自己建造就好了。不合逻辑的是，当这种做法走得太远时，人们就拒绝接受资本主义固有的劳动分工，或者拒绝向他人学习的想法。

一般来说，最好将这些政治范式视为编程范式。就像有时需要递归方法与命令式方法一样，有时也需要自给自足的方法。但你不必将自己视为递归主义者或自给自足主义者。只需使用可用的最佳工具即可。

理论上还有第三条道路，既不是扩张主义的，也不是自给自足的。做一个“正常”的国家，与他人进行贸易，既不征服也不仇恨他们！但实际上，这意味着你要么是一个帝国的一部分，要么你足够自给自足，可以在该帝国之外生存。

items.title:
You know how socialists don't get capitalism?
Well, nationalists don't get diplomacy.

The socialist thinks their state is all-powerful domestically, and the nationalist thinks their state is all-powerful globally, so it can seize whatever it wants by force, because it's so strong, and nothing will ever happen, and no one can resist.

Both worldviews are wrong, for similar reasons.

The socialist hates capitalists so much, and the nationalist hates foreigners so much, that they often just become blinded by hatred. They come to believe the outgroup is so incompetent that they aren't a threat, and have no use, so long as maximum force is employed.

And sometimes this mindset takes them surprisingly far!
They loot or conquer much more than you'd expect.
But eventually, they encounter constraint.

items.title_zh:
你知道社会主义者怎么不会走向资本主义吗？
好吧，民族主义者得不到外交。

社会主义者认为他们的国家在国内是全能的，民族主义者认为他们的国家在全球是全能的，所以它可以用武力夺取任何它想要的东西，因为它太强大了，什么都不会发生，没有人可以抵抗。

出于类似的原因，这两种世界观都是错误的。

社会主义者如此憎恨资本家，民族主义者如此憎恨外国人，以至于他们常常被仇恨蒙蔽了双眼。他们开始相信外团体是如此无能，只要使用最大的武力，他们就不会构成威胁，也没有任何用处。

有时这种心态会让他们出人意料地走得更远！
他们掠夺或征服的东西比你想象的要多得多。
但最终，他们遇到了限制。

items.title:
Networks are a better mental model than states.

Within a state, there are many networks. From ideological groupings to digital communities. They aren’t internally homogenous.

Between states, the countries themselves belong to networks. Multilateral fora like ASEAN or WEF.

China is the closest exception to the rule. Perhaps the most internally homogenous large state, and least globally dependent on others. But even China isn’t fully an exception, if you look closely.

All these types of informal networks are becoming more important, relative to formal states, as the 20th century ends and the Internet century begins.

items.title_zh:
网络是比国家更好的思维模型。

在一个州内，有许多网络。从意识形态群体到数字社区。它们的内部并不是同质的。

在国家之间，国家本身就属于网络。东盟或世界经济论坛等多边论坛。

中国是最接近这一规则的例外。也许是内部最同质化的大国，并且在全球范围内对其他国家的依赖程度最低。但如果你仔细观察的话，即使是中国也不完全例外。

随着 20 世纪的结束和互联网世纪的开始，所有这些类型的非正式网络相对于正式国家都变得更加重要。
